events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to identify component/condition: missing knockout filler. Panelboard installation & makeup, rough-complete stage. Condition: installed-defective? Missing knockout filler means there is an opening in the panelboard where a knockout was removed but not filled with a proper closure plug or fitting. This is a violation of NEC 408.38 (Panelboards—Enclosure) which requires that enclosures be so constructed that no openings remain that would permit foreign matter to enter. Also 110.12(A) requires unused openings to be closed. So it's a defect. The work is rough-complete, so it's installed but not closed out. That means the missing filler is a non-compliant condition. Decision: fail because it's a safety hazard (potential for arc flash, foreign objects, fingers). Risk: medium or high? Missing knockout filler is a common violation, risk of shock or arc flash if live parts are exposed. In industrial setting, likely high. But I'll say medium because it can be fixed easily. Work stage: rough-complete (as given). component_conditions: ["installed-defective"] maybe "non-compliant". The condition is defective because it's missing a knockout filler. So "installed-defective" is appropriate. Percent complete: perhaps 95%? Since makeup done but missing plug. Value: null. Sound_source: null. Confidence: high. Event_time, rate, order, workable: not needed. findings: "Panelboard has an open knockout that is not filled. This leaves a gap in the enclosure, potentially exposing energized parts and allowing foreign matter ingress." actions: "Install a knockout filler or closure plug to seal the opening." rationale: "NEC 110.12(A) requires unused openings in enclosures to be closed effectively. Missing knockout filler is a code violation and creates a safety hazard." references: ["NEC 110.12(A)", "NEC 408.38"]. I'll include. Decision: fail. Risk: medium. Component conditions: ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"]. percent_complete: I'll estimate 95, because makeup is done except missing plug. Value: 0 (since no numeric reading). confidence: 0.95. event_time: 0, rate: 0, order: [], workable: [], findings: one string, actions: one string. That's it.

  "findings": [
    "Missing knockout filler creates an opening in the panelboard enclosure",
    "Open hole compromises the deadfront or enclosure integrity, allowing potential ingress of tools, debris, or vermin",
    "Violates NEC 408.7 requirement for enclosure to be effectively closed in completed installation"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install appropriate knockout plug or filler in the open hole immediately",
    "Inspect all other knockouts for missing closures",
    "Verify panelboard interior is free of foreign material before closing up"
  ],
  "rationale": "Even at rough-complete stage, an unsealed knockout opening in a panelboard enclosure is a defect because it exposes energized or potentially energized internal components, violates the listing and installation requirements of the equipment, and poses a shock or fire hazard. NEC 110.12(A) requires unused openings to be closed effectively. While working clearances (110.26) are not directly violated, the missing filler is a construction defect that must be corrected before the panelboard can be considered properly roughed in.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Art. 408",
    "NEC 110.12(A)",
    "NEC 110.26"
  ]
}
